title: Top 5 Geopolitics Headlines

slides.headline: Marine Le Pen faces key ruling
slides.explanation: A Paris court will decide on Tuesday if Marine Le Pen can run for president next year. The outcome could significantly change the future of French politics.

slides.headline: Canada picks Germany for sub deal
slides.explanation: Canada is buying 12 new submarines from a German company. This huge defense contract is expected to strengthen their ties with NATO.

slides.headline: Russia strikes Kyiv before summit
slides.explanation: Russia launched attacks on Kyiv just as a major NATO summit is about to begin. The ongoing conflict is once again taking center stage in international diplomacy.

slides.headline: New evidence in Charlie Kirk case
slides.explanation: Prosecutors are presenting more video footage in court today. They are trying to convince a judge to proceed with the trial of the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk.

slides.headline: The growing gap in air travel
slides.explanation: US airlines are focusing more on premium cabins to boost profits. This is creating a wider divide between the experiences of passengers on the same flight.

caption: From international defense deals to major political rulings, here is what you need to know today. We are breaking down the biggest global headlines to keep you informed. Tap through to catch up on the stories that matter. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

title: Top 5 Finance Stories Today

slides.headline: Chip sector optimism is back
slides.explanation: Micron stock is up, which has investors feeling good about the chip industry. People are now watching Samsung and SK Hynix to see if the momentum continues.

slides.headline: China pushes into robotaxis
slides.explanation: Chinese self-driving car companies are expanding worldwide. They are using the country's strong electric vehicle supply chain to get a head start.

slides.headline: Trump's new take on Walmart
slides.explanation: President Trump recently called Walmart patriotic for lowering beef prices. This is a big shift from last year when he criticized the company over tariffs.

slides.headline: AI fears hit Asian markets
slides.explanation: Asian stocks dipped today as investors worried about high AI valuations. Even strong earnings from Samsung were not enough to lift the mood.

slides.headline: Amazon apologizes over course
slides.explanation: Amazon blocked a breastfeeding mother from a business course because of its site policies. The company has since apologized for not explaining the rules clearly.

caption: Here is your quick look at today's top finance stories. From shifting market trends to major corporate updates, we have got you covered. Stay informed without the noise with Headlinne. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
